title: hymenolyma bupleuroides
intro: Hymenolyma bupleuroides is a plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is native to New Zealand and has been used traditionally in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to help in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting off infections. Hymenolyma bupleuroides can be prepared as an infusion or a tincture, and its leaves and roots are used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.
